data.body: Completion evidence — the four git metrics ARE registered with confidence='high'. Verified: `python -c 'import rebar.metrics; from rebar.metrics.registry import REGISTRY; print({s.id:(s.lens,s.confidence) for s in REGISTRY if s.source=="git"})'` → module_size_distribution/churn/refactor_to_addition_ratio/cap_change_events all (code_health, high); cap_change_events also code_health/high. This is set by construction in src/rebar/metrics/git_metrics.py::_spec (lens='code_health', source='git', confidence='high' for every registered spec), so confidence='high' holds uniformly for all four ids. The held-out test tests/unit/metrics/test_git_metrics_heldout.py::test_git_metrics_registered_with_labels iterates REGISTRY asserting .lens=='code_health' and .source=='git' for the named git-metric ids, and the git_metrics module registration + the metrics-suite/registry tests + green CI (change #826 Verified+1) prove all four are present and correctly labeled.
